seo.title: Tool Loop Detection in AI Agents in Agno Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix tool loop detection in ai agents in Agno agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In Agno agents, tool loops manifest as infinite or near-infinite cycles where agents repeatedly call the same sequence of tools without progressing toward task completion, often triggered by ambiguous tool responses or missing termination conditions in tool chains. This is particularly prevalent in multi-modal agents processing images/audio where tool outputs create feedback loops, and in team agents where sub-agents pass malformed results between each other. Agno's lightweight decorator-based architecture makes these loops hard to detect without explicit monitoring of tool call sequences.

howItManifests.symptom: Tool call stack shows repeating pattern: describe_image → search_web → describe_image
howItManifests.impact: Agent consumes 100+ tool calls without generating final response, hitting rate limits

howItManifests.scenario: Team agent where worker1 calls 'validate_data' → worker2 calls 'validate_data' → worker1
howItManifests.symptom: Cross-agent tool calls exceed 50 iterations with no coordinator intervention
howItManifests.impact: Contradictory sub-results accumulate, coordinator fails to synthesize response

howItManifests.symptom: Memory tool calls > 20 with identical query patterns in session history
howItManifests.impact: Fabricated memory entries created, agent hallucinates past interactions

howItManifests.symptom: Schema validation tool called > 15 times with same validation errors
howItManifests.impact: Never produces valid structured output, session times out after 10 minutes

detection.name: Tool Call Sequence Tracker
detection.description: Monitors tool call history for repeating patterns longer than 5 calls using Agno's session traces
detection.code:
from collections import deque
import agno

def detect_tool_loop(agent):
    call_history = deque(maxlen=20)
    
    original_run = agent.run
    
    def wrapped_run(*args, **kwargs):
        result = original_run(*args, **kwargs)
        
        # Extract tool calls from Agno session trace
        tools_used = [step.tool for step in agent.session.trace if hasattr(step, 'tool')]
        call_history.extend(tools_used[-3:])
        
        # Detect repeating pattern
        if len(call_history) >= 6:
            pattern = tuple(call_history[-3:] * 2)
            if pattern[:3] == pattern[3:]:
                raise Exception(f'Tool loop detected: {list(call_history[-3:])} repeating')
        
        return result
    
    agent.run = wrapped_run
    return agent

detection.name: Max Tool Calls Guard
detection.description: Hard limit of 15 tool calls per session with Agno's built-in metrics tracking

detection.name: Agent State Cycle Detector
detection.description: Tracks agent internal state hashes to detect when state repeats across tool calls

fixes.name: Tool Call Blacklist
fixes.description: Prevents recently used tools from being called again within window using Agno tool registry
fixes.code:
from collections import deque
import agno

def add_loop_prevention(agent):
    recent_tools = deque(maxlen=3)
    
    original_select_tool = agent._select_tool
    
    def loop_safe_tool_selection(tools, *args, **kwargs):
        available_tools = [t for t in tools if t.name not in recent_tools]
        if not available_tools:
            # Force human intervention or final answer
            return None
        
        selected = original_select_tool(available_tools, *args, **kwargs)
        if selected:
            recent_tools.append(selected.name)
        return selected
    
    agent._select_tool = loop_safe_tool_selection
    return agent

fixes.name: Progressive Tool Cost
fixes.description: Increases 'cost' of repeated tools to bias LLM away from loops in Agno's tool selection

fixes.name: Loop Escape Prompts
fixes.description: Injects loop-breaking system prompts when patterns detected in Agno agent context
fixes.code:
import agno

def loop_escape_prompts(agent):
    def inject_escape_prompt(context):
        tool_pattern = [step.tool for step in agent.session.trace[-4:]]
        if len(set(tool_pattern[-3:])) <= 1:
            context.system += "\n\n[URGENT] You are in a tool loop. STOP calling the same tool. " \
                            "Either give final answer or use COMPLETELY DIFFERENT tool. " \
                            "Do not repeat last 3 tools used."
        return context
    
    original_context = agent._build_context
    agent._build_context = lambda: inject_escape_prompt(original_context())
    return agent

vexAutomation.heading: Automating Tool Loop Detection in AI Agents Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex provides production-grade tool loop detection for Agno agents through its verify decorator, automatically learning failure patterns from your agent sessions without manual configuration. It monitors Agno's session traces in real-time, detecting cycles in tool call sequences, state repetition, and excessive tool usage across single agents and teams. When loops are detected, Vex automatically applies context-aware fixes including tool blacklisting, escape prompts, and progressive costs, then verifies remediation success before resuming normal operation.

The integration wraps Agno's agent.run() method and works seamlessly with multi-modal and team agents. Vex maintains a rolling baseline of healthy tool call patterns per agent/task type, flagging deviations as loops. Remediation is automatic with fallback to human review only for novel failure modes, ensuring 99.9% uptime while eliminating manual monitoring overhead.

Vex also provides rich observability dashboards showing loop frequency by tool/agent, MTTR metrics, and auto-generated remediation templates for your Agno deployments.
vexAutomation.code:
import vex
from agno import Agent

# Install: pip install agno vex-sdk

@vex.verify(
    failure_modes=['tool_loop'],
    max_tool_calls=15,
    detect_cycles=True,
    auto_remediate=True
)
def reliable_agent_task():
    # Your Agno agent
    image_analyzer = Agent(
        name="ImageAnalyzer",
        tools=[describe_image, search_web, generate_json],
        model="gpt-4o"
    )
    
    result = image_analyzer.run("Analyze this chart: [image_url]")
    return result

# Vex automatically:
# 1. Detects tool loops in real-time
# 2. Applies fixes (blacklist, escape prompts)
# 3. Verifies remediation success
# 4. Falls back to human if needed
# 5. Logs patterns for future prevention

cta.heading: Detect and Fix This Automatically with Vex
